response.justification: The candidate is an accomplished generalist software engineer with strong experience in TypeScript/Node.js, DLang, and distributed web/microservices systems, but the role centers on hands-on C++ development for industrial automation. C++ exposure is minimal and dated (roughly six months at National Instruments in 2014–2015 plus a bachelor thesis), and there is no evidence of STL-heavy OOP work, MQTT/IoT, message brokers, or the required IEC 62443 / Secure Development Lifecycle background. Despite ample years of experience and solid engineering practices (TDD, CI/CD), the core technical alignment is too weak for a C++-focused senior position.
strengths: 13+ years of broad engineering experience with strong craftsmanship (TDD, CI/CD)
Proven low-latency, high-throughput distributed/microservices systems work
Some C/C++ background (National Instruments, C++ thesis project)
concerns: C++ experience is minimal and roughly a decade out of date
No IEC 62443, SDL, MQTT, or industrial-automation/IoT exposure
No message-broker or industrial-protocol (Modbus, OPC UA, DNP3) experience

key_factors.factor: Strong general engineering and distributed-systems track record
